amount.description: Amount of time off taken. This is the total amount of time off taken. For example, if you take 2 days off, this will be 2. If you take 1 day off, this will be 1.

is_paid.description: Whether the time off is paid or unpaid.

time_off_dailies.description: Used to break down the time off request into daily records. Each entry represents a single day within the time off period, allowing for detailed tracking of working days, non-working days, and holidays included in the request.

time_off_type_id.description: Time off type id. This is the id of the time off type associated with the time off request.

recipient_profile.description: The time off profile of the recipient of the time off request. Must be a worker profile.

requester_profile.description: The time off profile of the requester of the time off request. Can be either a client or worker profile.

time_off_percentage.description: Used to specify the percentage of time off taken relative to full-time employment. This value ranges from 0 to 1, where 1 represents full-time (100%) and 0.5 represents part-time (50%). This is useful for part-time workers or when taking partial time off.

is_end_date_estimated.description: Used to indicate if the end date is an estimate rather than a confirmed date. This is particularly important for event-based policies such as Parental Leave, where the actual end date may depend on the actual birth or adoption date, which may not be known at the time of request submission.

other_type_description.description: Used to provide a custom description when the time off type is categorized as 'OTHER'. This allows users to specify the nature of time off that doesn't fit into standard categories, providing flexibility for unique or organization-specific time off types.

deelToken.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)
